title: Weekly Surge In Google Trends For Energy Bill Shock Signals Bearish Xle
thesis: A rise in searches for energy bill shock indicates consumer stress from rising energy costs, potentially reducing discretionary spending and pressuring energy sector stocks. Consumer stress related to energy costs weighs on demand and sentiment in the energy sector.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly data for 'energy bill shock'
signal_logic: Enter short when Google Trends index for 'energy bill shock' increases by more than 15% week-over-week
entry_exit: Entry: Enter short when Google Trends index for 'energy bill shock' increases by more than 15% week-over-week Exit: Exit after 3 weeks or when the index falls below the 4-week moving average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly data for 'energy bill shock' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Energy prices fluctuate frequently causing repeated consumer stress search spikes.

schema.idea: Weekly surge in Google Trends for 'energy bill shock' signals bearish XLE

data.source: Google Trends weekly data for 'energy bill shock'

schema.behavior: A rise in searches for energy bill shock indicates consumer stress from rising energy costs, potentially reducing discretionary spending and pressuring energy sector stocks.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer stress related to energy costs weighs on demand and sentiment in the energy sector.

trade_logic.entry: Enter short when Google Trends index for 'energy bill shock' increases by more than 15% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 3 weeks or when the index falls below the 4-week moving average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Energy prices fluctuate frequently causing repeated consumer stress search spikes.

title: Daily Jump In Freight Railcar Loadings Signals Bullish Xli
thesis: A sudden increase in railcar loadings implies rising industrial demand and shipment activity, signaling strength in industrials. Increased freight railcar usage precedes industrial production and capital goods demand.
signal_logic: Enter long if daily railcar loadings increase by more than 5% from prior day
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long if daily railcar loadings increase by more than 5% from prior day Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if loadings decline 3 days in a row
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED weekly railcar loadings data via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Railcar loadings fluctuate frequently with industrial activity shifts.

trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if loadings decline 3 days in a row

title: Daily Spike In Political Insider Filings Selling Financial Stocks Signals Bearish Xlf

entry_exit: Entry: Enter short if daily filings showing insider sales in XLF stocks exceed 2 standard deviations above 30-day average Exit: Exit after 5 trading days or when filings normalize
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Political insider filing daily reports via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Political insider filings regularly fluctuate with market events.
